summary: Controls the SwitchBack policy under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat. SwitchBack allows applications that detect Windows Vista or later to also receive compatibility fixes normally reserved for older OS versions. SbEnable=0 disables this behavior. Extracted from privacy.disable-appcompat-engine.policy on 2026-03-13 because SbEnable is individually proven and does not carry the Microsoft-documented BSOD risk tied to DisableEngine in the parent bundle. AppCompat.admx has explicit enabledValue=0 and disabledValue=1 for SbEnable, the Microsoft Learn CSP page confirms the policy exists, and Procmon confirmed svchost.exe (gpsvc) writes SbEnable=REG_DWORD 0x0 during gpupdate /force on Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200.8037.

setting.name: Disable SwitchBack Compatibility Policy
setting.casual_explanation: SwitchBack is a Windows compatibility feature that lets newer applications receive compatibility fixes originally designed for older OS versions. On a modern Windows 11 system running only current software, this feature usually has no practical effect. This policy disables it.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable SwitchBack when they want a leaner compatibility policy surface and run no legacy software. They leave it enabled when they run older applications that may benefit from cross-version compatibility fixes.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling SwitchBack has no known BSOD risk on a system running only current software. Unlike DisableEngine, Microsoft documentation does not associate SbEnable=0 with engine-wide compatibility breakage.
setting.professional_notes: SbEnable lives at HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat. Policy family: AppCompat.admx. AppCompat.admx has explicit enabledValue=0 and disabledValue=1 for SbEnable, so numeric direction is proven without relying on context inference. Microsoft Learn CSP page (policy-csp-admx-appcompat) confirms AppCompatTurnOffSwitchBack exists and is device-scoped on Windows 11 21H2+. Procmon confirmed runtime write on Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200.8037 (2026-03-13). Split from privacy.disable-appcompat-engine.policy on 2026-03-13 to allow independent publication separate from the BSOD-risk DisableEngine value.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows keeps the normal SwitchBack behavior. Applications that detect Windows Vista or later can receive compatibility fixes designed for older OS versions.

allowed_values.label: SwitchBack enabled (policy explicit)
allowed_values.meaning: disabledValue in AppCompat.admx. SwitchBack runs normally. Equivalent to missing.

allowed_values.label: Turn off SwitchBack
allowed_values.meaning: enabledValue in AppCompat.admx. SwitchBack is disabled. Applications that detect Windows Vista or later no longer receive cross-version compatibility fixes. Procmon confirmed svchost.exe (gpsvc) writes SbEnable=REG_DWORD 0x0 during gpupdate /force on Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200.8037 (2026-03-13).

targets.notes: Windows default is missing (key absent). Missing behaves identically to value=1. Explicit enabledValue=0 in AppCompat.admx proves numeric direction without context inference. No BSOD risk is documented for SbEnable alone; that warning belongs to DisableEngine in the parent bundle.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app writes SbEnable=0 as part of the broader application-compatibility policy bundle. This record documents SbEnable in isolation after extraction from privacy.disable-appcompat-engine.policy. Path and value match local AppCompat.admx and Procmon capture. The app still writes DisableEngine=1 in the same code path; that value is tracked separately in privacy.disable-appcompat-engine.policy.

states.rationale: Key absent. SwitchBack runs in its default enabled state.

recommended_profiles.label: Turn off SwitchBack
intended_for: Windows 11 users running only current software
Users who want a reduced compatibility policy surface
avoid_for: Systems running legacy applications that depend on cross-version compatibility fixes

states.rationale: enabledValue confirmed in AppCompat.admx. Procmon confirmed runtime write on Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200.8037 (2026-03-13).

recommended_profiles.good_when: Running a clean Windows 11 system with only current software. SwitchBack usually has no practical effect in this scenario.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: Running older applications that may rely on cross-version compatibility fix delivery.
tradeoffs: Removes SwitchBack compatibility fix delivery for applications that detect Vista or later.
No known BSOD risk; that warning is specific to DisableEngine, not SbEnable.
Reversible by deleting the value or setting it to 1 and refreshing policy.
recommended_profiles.notes: apply_allowed=true. Proof chain is complete: AppCompat.admx explicit enabledValue=0, Microsoft Learn CSP page, and Procmon runtime proof on Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200.8037 (2026-03-13). No BSOD risk is documented for SbEnable alone.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows managed default
intended_for: All users who want normal Windows compatibility behavior

states.rationale: Leave policy unset. SwitchBack runs normally.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to provide full compatibility assistance including SwitchBack.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to reduce compatibility policy surface on a modern-only system.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft AppCompat.admx mappings
evidence.summary: Maps SbEnable with explicit enabledValue=0 and disabledValue=1 under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat in AppCompatTurnOffSwitchBack. Numeric direction is fully proven without context inference.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft AppCompat.adml help text
evidence.summary: Describes SwitchBack behavior as allowing apps that detect Vista or later to receive compatibility fixes for older OS versions. No BSOD warning is attached to SbEnable; the risk text in this file is specific to DisableEngine.

evidence.title: Policy CSP - ADMX_AppCompat
evidence.summary: Microsoft Learn CSP page confirming AppCompatTurnOffSwitchBack exists, is device-scoped, and applies to Windows 11 21H2+. Raw REG_DWORD values still come from the local ADMX.

evidence.title: Procmon + LGPO capture - AppCompatTurnOffSwitchBack
evidence.summary: LGPO.exe applied SbEnable=0 via local policy. Procmon captured svchost.exe -k GPSvcGroup (gpsvc) writing HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat\SbEnable=REG_DWORD 0x0 during gpupdate /force. reg query confirmed the value at the expected path. Rollback deleted the value and gpupdate /force returned the key to its clean baseline. Capture file: C:\Users\<USER>\AppData\Local\Temp\appcompat_policy_capture_lgpo_clean.pml

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: Writes SbEnable=0 as part of the application-compatibility policy bundle.

decision.why: Proof chain is complete and no comparable BSOD or engine-wide compatibility risk is documented for SbEnable. AppCompat.admx provides explicit enabledValue=0, the Microsoft Learn CSP page confirms policy existence and scope, and Procmon confirmed the runtime write. recommended_for_general_users remains false because the practical benefit is small and most users do not need to manage this policy explicitly.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: AppCompat.admx: `<policy name="AppCompatTurnOffSwitchBack" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat" valueName="SbEnable">`; AppCompat.adml: `If you enable this policy setting, Switchback will be turned off.` and `If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the Switchback will be turned on.`
validation_proof.notes: Local official AppCompat.admx and AppCompat.adml files document the exact SbEnable policy surface and semantics, and Procmon confirmed gpupdate writes SbEnable=REG_DWORD 0x0 on Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200.8037.
